app.name: Wix Scanner
app.tagline: Fast, reliable event check-in for Wix-powered venues

layout.title: Project skeleton ready
layout.description: Backend health, translated UI strings, and the operator shell are wired for rapid feature work.

home.title: Welcome
home.description: This starter uses Spanish by default and includes shadcn-style building blocks.

hero.title: Run check-in from one clear command center
hero.description: Monitor event readiness, integration health, and webhook flow without opening developer panels.

dashboard.quickActionsDescription: Jump to scanning, refresh integrations, and review latest deliveries.
dashboard.healthDescription: A compact overview of what needs attention before doors open.

helpModal.title: Understanding the Dashboard
helpModal.subtitle: A guide to the readiness indicators and quick actions.
helpModal.step1Body: The metrics on the right show verified events, scope warnings, and failed deliveries. All-zero badges mean you are ready to scan.
helpModal.step2Title: Use Quick Actions before doors open
helpModal.step2Body: Refresh Integrations pulls the latest Wix site bindings. Refresh Deliveries syncs webhook history. Use Open Operator Screen to launch the kiosk view.
helpModal.step3Title: Navigate to the right tab if something is wrong
helpModal.step3Body: Scope warnings → go to Integrations. Webhook issues → go to Deliveries. For a full diagnostic report, go to Readiness.
helpModal.step4Body: Start in API Key Management to connect your Wix credentials, then visit Integrations to bind a site and event, and finally use Readiness to confirm you are ready.

webhook.title: Webhook Delivery History
webhook.description: Monitor Wix mobile check-in webhooks and trigger manual retries for failed deliveries.

helpModal.title: Webhook Delivery Records
helpModal.subtitle: How to understand and act on Wix check-in webhook deliveries.
helpModal.step1Body: Each row is one Wix mobile check-in event sent to the scanner. The status field shows whether the record was processed, failed, or is queued for retry.
helpModal.step2Body: Retry a failed delivery if the backend was temporarily unavailable. Retries are idempotent — a ticket already checked in will not be double-processed.
helpModal.step3Title: How to find the Wix event ID
helpModal.step3Body: The event field in each row matches the Wix event ID visible in your Wix Dashboard under Events > select your event > copy the ID from the URL.
helpModal.step4Body: If no deliveries appear, check that your Wix site is bound in the Integrations tab and that the scanner app is installed and active on that Wix site.
helpModal.referenceEvents: Events API — List Events (Wix API Docs)

bindings.title: Site-Event Binding Admin
bindings.description: Bind Wix sites to events, verify app installation, and only activate verified events.
bindings.activateError: Activation blocked: event has no verified binding

bindingHelp.title: How to complete site-event binding
bindingHelp.subtitle: Guide based on Wix MCP flows and Wix API references.
bindingHelp.step1Body: Use Wix MCP ListWixSites (or Wix dashboard site info) and copy the exact site ID for the event owner site.
bindingHelp.step2Body: Use Wix MCP events listing (Query/List Events) and copy the target event ID. Use the exact event used for check-in.
bindingHelp.step3Body: Enter site-id and event-id in this screen, then create binding. Run Verify to confirm site/event/app installation state.
bindingHelp.step4Body: Run scope verification after binding is verified. Required permissions are WIX_EVENTS.READ_TICKETS, WIX_EVENTS.CHECK-IN, and WIX_EVENTS.READ_EVENTS.

credentials.title: Credential Lifecycle
credentials.description: Manage Wix API credential profiles, validate connectivity, and control auth-mode decisions across environments.
credentials.rotateSuccess: Credential rotated — new profile is now active

helpModal.title: How to verify the Wix app instance
helpModal.subtitle: Use the real Wix Get App Instance method before you validate or rotate credentials.
helpModal.step1Body: Use Wix MCP to call Get App Instance for the target site. The method requires Wix app or Wix user authentication and does not take request parameters.
helpModal.step2Body: Confirm the response includes the installed app instance and site information, such as instanceId, appName, siteId, and siteDisplayName.
helpModal.step3Body: If the app instance looks wrong or the app is not installed, fix that first. Then continue with credential validation or rotation.
helpModal.referenceBody: Get App Instance retrieves data about the app instance installed on a Wix site and the site itself.
helpModal.referenceLink: Open the Wix Get App Instance docs

validateModal.title: Validate Wix API Credential
validateModal.subtitle: Use Wix MCP to confirm your credential is correctly configured before running validation.
validateModal.step1Body: Check the auth mode of this credential (OAuth or API Key). For production, only OAuth is permitted.
validateModal.step2Body: Use Wix MCP 'Get App Instance' to confirm the Wix app is installed on the target site and the credential is still valid.
validateModal.step3Body: Click Confirm to call the Wix API. The system will contact the Wix platform and update the credential state based on the response.

rotateModal.title: Rotate Credential
rotateModal.subtitle: Rotating creates a new credential, validates it, and revokes the old one. Use Wix MCP to prepare before confirming.
rotateModal.step1Body: Use Wix MCP 'Get App Instance' or the Wix dashboard to retrieve the new API token or OAuth credentials for the replacement profile.
rotateModal.step2Body: For production environments, OAuth is required. Confirm the auth mode before rotating to avoid mixed-mode violations.
rotateModal.step3Body: The system will create and validate the new credential. If validation succeeds, the old credential is revoked. If it fails, the old credential stays active.
rotateModal.step4Body: After rotation, verify auth-mode consistency if running in production. Mixed modes are blocked in production.

authSettings.title: Authentication Settings
authSettings.description: Inspect token-mode health, run manual token refresh, and test Wix connectivity without exposing token values.

helpModal.title: OAuth Token Lifecycle
helpModal.subtitle: How to monitor token health and recover from expiry.
helpModal.step1Body: api_key mode uses a static Wix API key managed in the API Key Management tab. oauth mode uses a short-lived OAuth token that must be refreshed periodically.
helpModal.step2Body: Click 'Refresh token' if the token status shows expired or invalid. The system will request a new access token from Wix automatically.
helpModal.step3Body: Click 'Test connection' to verify the current token can reach Wix without making any changes. A green result means you are ready.
helpModal.step4Body: Consider switching to api_key auth mode for long-running kiosk deployments. API keys do not expire on their own and require less maintenance.

apiKeySettings.title: API Key Management
apiKeySettings.description: Enter the Wix API key and account ID, test the connection first, and save only after validation succeeds.
apiKeySettings.saveError: Could not save API key settings
apiKeySettings.loadError: Could not load API key settings

helpModal.title: How to collect the API key and account ID
helpModal.subtitle: Use Wix MCP and the Wix dashboard to verify the right site context before saving API key mode settings.
helpModal.step1Title: Confirm the Wix site and app instance
helpModal.step1Body: Open Wix MCP Get App Instance to confirm the site context, installed app instance, and the app/site pairing for the venue.
helpModal.step2Title: Get the API key from Wix
helpModal.step2Body: Use the Wix dashboard or your secret manager to retrieve the API key for this environment. The key is stored locally after validation and never displayed back.
helpModal.step3Body: Copy the Wix account ID from the account context used for this venue. If your setup uses account-scoped MCP headers, keep that value aligned with the same Wix account.
helpModal.step4Body: Paste both values, run Test connection, and only save after the connection succeeds. If the site/app context is wrong, fix that first in Wix.
helpModal.referenceBody: These links match the flow used by the backend validation path and Wix MCP guidance.

readiness.title: Event Readiness
readiness.description: Check bindings, credentials, scopes, manifest freshness, cache warming, backend connectivity, and worker health before activation.
readiness.acknowledge: I acknowledge degraded readiness and want to activate anyway.
readiness.empty: Enter an event ID to evaluate readiness.

helpModal.title: Event Readiness Checks
helpModal.subtitle: What each component status means and how to resolve failures.
helpModal.step1Body: Find your event ID in the Wix Dashboard under Events. Select your event and copy the alphanumeric ID from the URL (e.g. /events/abc123).
helpModal.step2Body: 'Ready' means all checks passed. 'Degraded' means at least one non-critical check failed but scanning can still work. 'Critical' means a required check failed — scanning is not safe.
helpModal.step3Body: Binding — go to Integrations and verify the site+event pair. Credentials — go to Credentials or API Key Management. Scopes — re-verify app installation. Manifest — click Sync Manifest to refresh the ticket cache.
helpModal.step4Body: You can activate a degraded event by checking the acknowledgment box. Use this only when you accept the risk. Critical events cannot be activated.

syncControls.title: Wix Synchronization Controls
syncControls.description: Configure per-event sync cadence, enable or pause worker sync, and review latest sync health.
syncControls.empty: Select an event and load sync settings.

helpModal.title: Wix Synchronization Controls
helpModal.subtitle: How to set the sync interval and understand sync health.
helpModal.step1Body: The sync worker periodically fetches the latest ticket list from Wix and updates the local manifest cache. This keeps offline scanning accurate even when the backend is temporarily unreachable.
helpModal.step2Body: Use 1 minute for high-turnover events. Use 2 minutes for stable events with slower attendee flow. Shorter intervals mean fresher data but more Wix API calls.
helpModal.step3Body: Disable sync before planned maintenance or when testing to avoid unexpected ticket cache updates. Re-enable sync before scanning begins.
helpModal.step4Body: High lag means the last successful sync was a long time ago. Check backend connectivity and confirm the worker is running in the Readiness tab.
helpModal.referenceEvents: Events API — List Events (Wix API Docs)

eventConfig.title: Event Block Configuration
eventConfig.description: Create and manage scan windows (blocks) for your Wix events.
eventConfig.resetConfirmDescription: This will permanently clear all check-in records for this scope. Enter a reason to proceed.

helpModal.title: Event Block Configuration Guide
helpModal.subtitle: How to create events, configure scan windows, and set grace periods.
helpModal.step1Body: Open your Wix Dashboard, go to Events, select the event, and copy the ID from the browser URL bar. It looks like: /events/abc-123-def456.
helpModal.step2Body: Each block represents a time-boxed scan window (e.g. 'Morning Entry' or 'VIP Lane'). Use a short block code with no spaces and a descriptive block name.
helpModal.step3Body: The grace period is extra minutes added after the official block end time before the scanner starts rejecting tickets. Set 0 for strict cutoff.
helpModal.step4Body: Priority controls scan order when a ticket matches multiple blocks. Lower number means higher priority. Use 100 as a safe default.
helpModal.referenceEvents: Events API — List Events (Wix API Docs)
helpModal.referenceSupport: Creating a Wix Event (Wix Support Center)

reconciliation.title: Reconciliation
reconciliation.description: Compare local ticket state with Wix source-of-truth, apply deterministic drift resolution, and manually resolve conflicts.

helpModal.title: Reconciliation Guide
helpModal.subtitle: How to detect and resolve drift between local records and Wix.
helpModal.step1Body: Run after a scanning session ends or if the local check-in count does not match Wix. Reconciliation compares each ticket status locally vs Wix and identifies discrepancies.
helpModal.step2Body: 'In sync' means local and Wix match. 'Local pending' means a scan was processed locally but not yet confirmed in Wix. 'Wix only' means Wix has a check-in the local system missed. 'Conflict' requires manual resolution.
helpModal.step3Body: For each conflict, choose 'Accept Wix' to trust the Wix record, or 'Keep local' to trust the local scan. Wix is the source of truth for paid events.
helpModal.step4Title: How to find your Wix event ID
helpModal.step4Body: Open your Wix Dashboard, go to Events, select the event, and copy the ID from the browser URL (e.g. /events/abc-123).
helpModal.referenceEvents: Events API — List Events (Wix API Docs)

operator.title: Operator screen
operator.description: This route is ready for the kiosk UI and scanner workflow.
operator.idleMessage: Please present your QR code or ticket to the scanner
operator.emptyHistory: No scans in this session yet
operator.focusLost: Window focus was lost. Keep operator screen active.
operator.webhidUnavailable: WebHID is unavailable. Keyboard capture fallback is active.
operator.manifestStale: Local ticket cache is stale. Validation is degraded.
operator.offlineQueued: Backend unavailable. Scan marked as offline queue.

bootstrap.waitingSubtitle: Present the bootstrap QR to the scanner to activate this kiosk
bootstrap.sessionCleared: Session cleared. Scan bootstrap QR to re-activate.
bootstrap.sessionExpired: Kiosk session expired. Scan the bootstrap QR to re-activate.
bootstrap.notEnrolledError: Kiosk is not enrolled. Scan the bootstrap QR first.

toasts.welcome: Sonner is ready for operator notifications.
